expectations.evidence: opencollection.yml exists with info.name, info.version, info.type: collection, settings, and runtime sections
5-lifecycle folder chains authorize->token->userinfo->revoke->verify via bru.setVar: lcAuthCode in 5.1, lcAccessToken/lcRefreshToken in 5.2, used in 5.3/5.4/5.5
1-authorize/1.1-happy-path.yml: POST /oauth/authorize with assertions on res.body.code (string, length>0) and res.body.state matching expected value
2-token-exchange/2.1-happy-path.yml: POST /oauth/token with grant_type authorization_code, asserts access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type Bearer
3-userinfo/3.1-happy-path.yml: GET /oauth/userinfo; folder.yml injects Bearer token via bru.getVar('accessToken') in before-request script
4-revoke/4.1-happy-path.yml: POST /oauth/revoke with token body, asserts status 204
4-revoke/4.2-verify-revoked-token-fails.yml: GET /oauth/userinfo with revoked token, asserts status 401 and error property. Also 5-lifecycle/5.5 does the same.
environments/local.yml: clientId uses '{{process.env.CLIENT_ID}}' and clientSecret uses '{{process.env.CLIENT_SECRET}}'; .env.sample documents CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET placeholders
